hard.next_step: RFT to be released in July 2026 via Buying for Victoria tender site (no fixed date yet); Rachel Leng confirmed an update on WorkSafe's plans expected the week of 7 Jul 2026 (Next Step 6 Jul 2026, confidence 25%).
hard.competitor: Oracle Procurement Cloud (incumbent being replaced); unknown Buying-for-Victoria RFT respondents; status-quo / do-nothing

north_star_verdict.headline: EOI submitted 1 Jun 2026 and a July RFT is expected via Buying for Victoria, but the RFT keeps slipping with 'no date yet', Rachel Leng cited unfinished internal approvals (2 Jul 2026), and rep confidence was cut to 25% - the buyer is engaged but stalled on its own approvals with no champion driving it.
north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 154 (close 2026-12-07). Time in Qualified stage: 381 days (created and qualified 20 Jun 2025). EOI released 27 Jan 2026, submitted 1 Jun 2026; RFT deferred to July 2026 with no fixed date (Next Step 6 Jul 2026). The Dec 2026 close is not credible: RFT has not released, and Karen Bingham framed the full multi-stage cycle (EOI eval to RFQ/RFP to governance approval to award) plus ~6-month implementation as 'at least another 12 months' (1 Dec 2025). Account reassigned Adam Hasan to Luke Dougherty on 19 Jun 2026, restarting the buyer relationship. Pace is government-controlled and currently thinning.
evidence: Buyer engagement is thinning and waiting on WorkSafe's own approvals: Rachel Leng replied 2 Jul 2026 that WorkSafe is 'still finalising the internal approvals required for our market approach and next steps' and apologised for the delay - a stalled action awaiting approval, not fresh momentum.
The RFT has been deferred to July 2026 with 'no date yet' and rep confidence dropped to 25% (Next Step 6 Jul 2026), so the near-term milestone is undated and slipping.

No WorkSafe stakeholder is advocating for Zycus. Karen Bingham runs a deliberately fair tender ('I just want to be mindful that there is not any unforeseen advantage to any organization', 1 Dec 2025) and declined the offered RFP template, so we have no inside track.

Economic Buyer Matthew Mazzotta (CPO, Executive Sponsor per OpportunityContactRole) has been silent since the 9 Jul 2025 demo where he was in the room, leaving no active executive sponsorship as the RFT approaches.

Positive base: we displaced Oracle on the AI/productivity gap ('something more AI driven, more automated, something a bit more easier to use', Karen 1 Dec 2025), Merlin Intake was an 'eye opener' for the CPO, and Zycus is proven across Victorian government (Dept Transport, Justice, Environment, Treasury & Finance; Merlin Intake live at City of Melbourne and V/Line per Ashish, 9 Jul 2025).
The account changed hands on 19 Jun 2026 (Adam Hasan to Luke Dougherty), and Luke is re-introducing himself cold to Rachel, so the relationship is effectively restarting.

metrics.narrative: The business problem is well articulated but not yet quantified in dollars this cycle. Karen Bingham said the driver is 'supporting the strategic objectives plan of the procurement / finance team' and 'removing a lot of manual style activities that they do today', with automation as the goal (1 Dec 2025). Matt Mazzotta described 'a lot of manual processing within our procurement space and our contract management space' and named contract leakage as a specific loss: 'we really need a supporting contract life cycle management system to help us get the benefits out of our supplier engagements, and to minimize any leakage from our contracts' (9 Jul 2025 demo). The pain is operational and strategic (manual, paper-based S2C on top of Oracle ERP for P2P), but no ROI, savings, headcount, or cycle-time figure has been stated by the buyer yet because the deal is pre-commercials - quantifying the value case is a ranked move.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is identified but passive. Matthew Mazzotta (CPO, Executive Sponsor per OpportunityContactRole) is the project's business owner: Karen Bingham said the team is 'reporting to Matt Mazzotta, who we're reporting to at the moment for this project' and that findings go 'back to our governance forums' for stage approvals (1 Dec 2025). Matt personally attended the 9 Jul 2025 demo ('we've got Matt, our chief procurement officer in the room'), confirming his authority and interest, but he has not engaged since. Funding is stage-gated: 'we only have at the moment funding to proceed to the end of the EOI process' (Karen, 1 Dec 2025), so RFP-stage budget requires a separate governance approval Matt controls. The gap is not identity but engagement - rebuilding CPO sponsorship before the RFT is the move.
sources: Avoma demo 9 Jul 2025 (Matthew Mazzotta in room)

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are visible in direction but not yet formally weighted. Confirmed asks: AI capabilities and roadmap ('one of our key project governance members is interested in seeing what capabilities prospective suppliers can provide in the AI space', Karen 1 Dec 2025); two-way integration with Oracle ('putting in a potential requirement to have integration potentially both ways to the Oracle system... that's imperative', Karen 1 Dec 2025); automation of manual/paper-based sourcing, evaluation and contract management; contract lifecycle management with obligations, alerts and supplier performance monitoring (Matt asked specifically for post-execution contract management on the 9 Jul 2025 demo, not contract authoring); and government compliance/security. Karen flagged the RFI as 'quite vanilla' (30 Jan 2026), so explicit scoring weights are not yet published. The formal criteria will land with the July RFT - getting Zycus differentiators reflected in that scoring is the lever.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer described a textbook multi-stage Victorian-government procurement and is running it exactly that way. Karen Bingham mapped it: 'we've got the remit at the moment to complete an EOI process... we do have to take back the next step approach per the findings of the EOI back to our governance forums, and then that would be seeking permission to move to the next stage... hypothetically if it's an RFQ or whatever, then post that we would go for a final business case to implement with the successful vendor' (1 Dec 2025). Actual sequence observed: intro 20 Jun 2025, demo 9 Jul 2025, EOI released 27 Jan 2026, EOI submitted 1 Jun 2026, RFT expected July 2026 via Buying for Victoria, then shortlist to demos/pilot to RFQ/RFP to governance approval to award. Funding is released stage-by-stage. Rachel noted the final contract will likely route through the DGS eServices vehicle in the second stage (1 Dec 2025). The process is clear; the risk is that each governance gate is a place we can be filtered out with no champion in the room.
sources: Avoma call 1 Dec 2025 (Karen Bingham, Rachel Leng)
Next Step History 20 Jun 2025 to 6 Jul 2026

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are largely unknown because the deal is early (pre-shortlist). What is known: WorkSafe procures through the Buying for Victoria tender portal (tenders.vic.gov.au), and Rachel Leng indicated the final contract will likely need to run through the DGS (Department of Government Services) eServices contract vehicle at the second stage ('the direction from DGS is all agencies use eServices when we do the final contract... for the second stage we'll start a conversation about the DGS eServices contract then', 1 Dec 2025). WorkSafe also referenced DocuSign/e-signing and records/document management systems it will name in requirements (1 Dec 2025). No MSA/SOW, legal, InfoSec, or procurement-channel detail exists yet; Zycus already holds Victorian-government security/compliance clearances from prior departments, which Luke offered as a de-risking proof. Visibility will come once the RFT defines the contracting path - confirming the DGS eServices route and standard terms is the move as the deal advances.
sources: Avoma call 1 Dec 2025 (Rachel Leng, Karen Bingham)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is specific and repeatedly confirmed by the buyer. WorkSafe runs S2C on manual, paper-based processes layered over Oracle ERP (used for P2P), which 'lacks the intelligence and productivity required' (Next Step 12 Jul 2025). Matt Mazzotta: 'there's a lot of manual processing within our procurement space and our contract management space', sourcing processes are 'quite paper based', and 'contract management is a set and forget' causing value and compliance leakage (9 Jul 2025 demo). Karen framed removal of 'manual style activities' and automation as the core driver (1 Dec 2025). Pain owners: Matt Mazzotta (CPO, accountable for the transformation) and the procurement/finance operating team (Mat Steenvoorden, transactional procurement). Confirmed pain: manual sourcing, weak contract lifecycle control, contract leakage. Inferred pain: supplier-risk and spend-visibility gaps (Adam probed these; buyer deferred them to 'phase 2/3').

champion.narrative: No champion exists. The two active buyer contacts, Karen Bingham (Project Manager) and Rachel Leng (Senior Category Advisor - ICT), are running a deliberately neutral, fair tender and explicitly avoid giving Zycus any edge: Karen declined the offered RFP template 'to be mindful that there is not any unforeseen advantage to any organization' and asked to be left off Zycus webinars until the EOI completes (1 Dec 2025). Neither has decision authority nor advocates internally. Matt Mazzotta (CPO/EB) is a structural Executive Sponsor, not a relational champion, and has been silent since 9 Jul 2025. Mat Steenvoorden (Decision Maker) and evaluators Simon Nelis, Cameron Peel and Erin Westcott engaged only at the intro/demo and have not re-surfaced. For an arm's-length $250k government tender with stage-gated funding and unseen RFT rivals, the absence of any coach is the single biggest structural weakness - cultivating Rachel or Karen into a coach who shares RFT timing/criteria is the highest-leverage champion move.
sources: Avoma call 1 Dec 2025 (Karen Bingham, Rachel Leng)

competition.narrative: This is effectively sole-source today with an open contest coming. Oracle Procurement Cloud is the incumbent being displaced (not a live bidder) because it 'lacks the intelligence and productivity required' and WorkSafe wants 'something more AI driven, more automated' (Karen 1 Dec 2025; Next Step 12 Jul 2025) - but Oracle Fusion ERP stays for P2P, so integration to Oracle is a hard requirement, not a competitive threat. No rival S2C vendor has been named across any call or Next Step. The real current threat is twofold: (1) unknown respondents to the open Buying-for-Victoria RFT in July 2026, where we have no visibility on who bids or how they price, and (2) do-nothing / status-quo, since funding is only secured through the EOI and each stage needs fresh governance approval, so a no-decision or deferral is a live risk. Our wedge is AI differentiation (Merlin Intake, agentic negotiation, contract/supplier-risk agents) mapped to WorkSafe's explicit AI requirement, plus a strong Victorian-government reference base that de-risks the Oracle-to-Zycus switch.

deal_movement.summary: A live but government-paced deal that is currently stalling on the buyer's own internal approvals. EOI was submitted 1 Jun 2026; the RFT has since been deferred to July 2026 with no fixed date and rep confidence cut to 25% (6 Jul 2026). The account was reassigned to Luke Dougherty on 19 Jun 2026, and the last real exchange (Rachel Leng, 2 Jul 2026) was a non-committal 'still finalising internal approvals' reply.

items.change: EOI submitted to WorkSafe; awaiting response and next steps by end of month
Account reassigned from Adam Hasan to Luke Dougherty (ANZ regional director) by Carl Kimball
Luke re-introduced himself as new owner; Rachel Leng replied WorkSafe is still finalising internal approvals for its market approach and next steps, apologising for the delay
Next Step updated: RFT to release in July via Buying for Victoria (no date yet), update on plans expected this week, confidence cut to 25%

competitive_position.summary: No live rival S2C vendor is named. Oracle Procurement Cloud is the incumbent being displaced on the AI/productivity gap (Karen 1 Dec 2025) but stays as the Oracle Fusion ERP that Zycus must integrate to, so it is a dormant incumbent, not a threat. The single strongest CURRENT threats are (1) unknown respondents to the open Buying-for-Victoria RFT expected July 2026, where an arm's-length process gives us no intel on who bids or how they price, and (2) do-nothing / status-quo, given stage-gated funding secured only through the EOI. We win on AI differentiation (Merlin Intake, agentic negotiation and contract/supplier-risk agents) mapped to WorkSafe's explicit AI requirement, plus a deep Victorian-government reference base.

competitors.name: Unknown RFT respondents
competitors.quote: RFT to be released July 2026 via Buying for Victoria tender site, no date yet (Next Step 6 Jul 2026); Karen: 'we are looking at issuing an expression of interest out into the market... release it to nominated or selected suppliers' (1 Dec 2025).
competitors.source: Avoma call 1 Dec 2025 (Karen Bingham); Next Step 6 Jul 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Lead the RFT response with quantified AI ROI (automation of manual sourcing, contract-leakage prevention, supplier risk) and the Victorian-government reference base (Dept Transport, Justice, Environment, Treasury & Finance; Merlin Intake live at City of Melbourne and V/Line) so an unseen rival cannot out-position us at governance or win purely on price.

competitors.name: Do nothing / status quo (retain Oracle + manual processes)
competitors.quote: 'We only have at the moment funding to proceed to the end of the EOI process' and 'the organization has certainly not made any decision at this stage as to what the direction will be' (Karen Bingham, 1 Dec 2025); each stage needs fresh governance approval.
competitors.how_we_win: Make the cost of inaction concrete for CPO Matt Mazzotta - manual sourcing and contract leakage against WorkSafe's spend - and secure the RFP-stage business case so the governance forum funds the next stage rather than deferring.

competitors.name: Oracle Procurement Cloud
competitors.quote: Oracle 'lacks the intelligence and productivity required, hence wanting to find out more about AI capabilities' (Next Step 12 Jul 2025); WorkSafe seeking 'something more AI driven, more automated, something a bit more easier to use' (Karen, 1 Dec 2025).
competitors.source: Avoma call 1 Dec 2025 (Karen Bingham); Next Step 12 Jul 2025
competitors.how_we_win: Oracle Fusion ERP stays for P2P, so lead with seamless two-way Oracle integration (Karen: integration to Oracle is 'imperative') plus the AI/automation Oracle procurement lacks - Merlin Intake, agentic negotiation and contract-lifecycle agents.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on AI, contract lifecycle and government implementation experience; the exposure is the fair-tender process itself, which limits how far ahead of the field we can get, and the still-unproven Oracle two-way integration.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers for a champion-less, arm's-length government tender with an imminent RFT.
flags: Cultivate Rachel Leng or Karen Bingham into a coach to learn RFT timing and evaluation criteria before release - no advocate exists in a fair tender.
Rebuild CPO Matthew Mazzotta's sponsorship (silent since 9 Jul 2025) with Victorian-government proof points before the RFT scoring is set.
We are blind on RFT competitors - lead the response with AI differentiation and quantified ROI tied to WorkSafe's manual-process and contract-leakage pain.

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Karen Bingham and Rachel Leng are neutral procurement professionals running a deliberately fair tender who decline to give Zycus any edge (Karen refused the RFP template and asked to be left off webinars, 1 Dec 2025). Matthew Mazzotta is a structural Executive Sponsor with no active advocacy and has been silent since 9 Jul 2025. No one is promoting Zycus internally or coaching Luke on how to win, which is the deal's biggest structural weakness ahead of the July RFT.
champion_strength.source: Avoma calls 1 Dec 2025 and 9 Jul 2025; OpportunityContactRole (no Champion role)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus is strongly positioned on AI: Merlin Intake (Teams-embedded conversational procurement) was an 'eye opener' for the CPO, and the platform showed agentic negotiation, AI contract review and supplier-risk agents. The story is landing; the risk is that WorkSafe's 'vanilla' RFI has no explicit AI scoring yet, so the differentiation is not yet formally rewarded.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 7 / Moderate is consistent with the evidence: WorkSafe explicitly wants AI capabilities (opp title is 'Agentic AI', Karen confirmed a governance member is interested in supplier AI capabilities) but takes a cautious public-sector posture ('uncertainty and cautious approach due to public sector', 1 Dec 2025) with no AI scoring criteria published yet. Genuine appetite, tempered by governance caution - neither over- nor under-rated by the field.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is thinning and rep-side: the last touch was a non-committal buyer email (Rachel, 2 Jul 2026) after Luke re-introduced himself as new owner, and rep confidence was cut to 25% with the RFT undated. Live but cooling toward the RFT gate.
items: Rachel Leng: 'still finalising the internal approvals... apologies for the delay' (2 Jul 2026)

scope_change.from: Full Source-to-Pay appetite (supplier management, spend/P2P and AI orchestration were all discussed; 'very good fit for a full S2P Suite', 30 Jan 2026)
scope_change.to: Source-to-Contract only, with supplier management, spend visibility and P2P deferred to 'phase 2/3'
scope_change.detail: Karen Bingham repeatedly bounded the buy: 'the focus is primarily on source to contract... the direction, I suppose, in terms of giving fair structure to everyone is the need for source to contract', deferring supplier management to a later phase (1 Dec 2025). Combined with stage-gated funding (only funded through the EOI), this is a defensive, cost-staged posture, not a full-suite commitment.

expansion_context.note: No sibling Closed-Won deal on WorkSafe Victoria; this is a net-new S2C pursuit, so the silent economic buyer and unbuilt executive access are genuine early-stage risks, not inherited access.

critical_signals.text: The last exchange was Rachel Leng's 2 Jul email saying WorkSafe is still finalising internal approvals and apologising for the delay, with the RFT still undated.
WorkSafe is running an open Buying-for-Victoria tender and has named no rival, so the real contest is unseen RFT respondents in July, not the displaced Oracle incumbent.
Luke Dougherty took over the account from Adam Hasan on 19 Jun 2026 and is re-introducing himself cold to Rachel, so the buyer relationship is effectively restarting.
Funding is stage-gated: WorkSafe is only funded through the EOI and must win separate governance approval for the RFP budget, so the $250k is not yet backed by committed spend.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We are the year-long incumbent-replacer with clear AI differentiation and a deep Victorian-government reference base, but this is an arm's-length open tender with no champion, a CPO sponsor silent for a year, and a July RFT that keeps slipping - the win hinges on getting intelligence and our AI story into the RFT before it releases.

win_position.text: We displaced Oracle a year ago on the AI/productivity gap ('something more AI driven, more automated', Karen 1 Dec 2025) and Merlin Intake was an 'eye opener' for CPO Matt Mazzotta, so we enter the July RFT as the intelligence-led front-runner.

No WorkSafe stakeholder advocates for Zycus - Karen Bingham runs a deliberately fair tender ('not any unforeseen advantage to any organization', 1 Dec 2025) and declined our RFP template, so we have no inside track on the RFT criteria.
Economic buyer Matt Mazzotta (CPO, Executive Sponsor) has been silent since the 9 Jul 2025 demo, leaving no executive sponsorship as the tender approaches.

Zycus is proven across Victorian government (Dept Transport, Justice, Environment, Treasury & Finance; Merlin Intake live at City of Melbourne and V/Line per Ashish, 9 Jul 2025), which de-risks the Oracle-to-Zycus switch.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is government-paced and thinning: Rachel Leng said WorkSafe is 'still finalising internal approvals' and apologised for the delay (2 Jul 2026), and the RFT has 'no date yet' with rep confidence cut to 25% (6 Jul 2026).
The account changed hands on 19 Jun 2026 (Adam Hasan to Luke Dougherty), so the buyer relationship is restarting just before the RFT.
The EOI was submitted 1 Jun 2026 and the RFT is expected in July, so the process is live and advancing rather than dead.

customer_commitment.text: WorkSafe is only funded through the EOI ('we only have funding to proceed to the end of the EOI process', Karen 1 Dec 2025); RFP-stage budget needs a separate governance approval.
The buyer has made no decision and is keeping it 'very very open' (Karen 1 Dec 2025), declined the RFP template and asked to be left off webinars - deliberately arm's-length.
WorkSafe invested in a full demo (9 Jul 2025) with the CPO in the room and scoped a full S2C suite, showing genuine intent to replace Oracle.

deal_risk.text: Scope narrowed from a 'full S2P suite' fit (30 Jan 2026) to S2C-only, with supplier and spend management deferred to 'phase 2/3' (Karen 1 Dec 2025) - a defensive, cost-staged posture.
The Dec 2026 close is not credible: the RFT has not released and Karen framed the full cycle as 'at least another 12 months' plus ~6-month implementation (1 Dec 2025).
We are blind on competitors - an open Buying-for-Victoria tender will draw unnamed rivals who could out-position us at governance or undercut on price.

champion_strength.evidence: No champion; Karen and Rachel are strictly neutral and declined any Zycus advantage (1 Dec 2025).

economic_buyer_engagement.evidence: CPO Matt Mazzotta silent since the 9 Jul 2025 demo; no active sponsorship.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer 'certainly not made any decisions', keeping it 'very very open' and process-fair (Karen 1 Dec 2025).

timeline_risk.evidence: RFT deferred with 'no date yet', confidence 25% (6 Jul 2026); Dec 2026 close not credible.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer keeping it 'very very open', 'certainly not made any decisions', declined the RFP template to keep the tender fair and asked to be left off webinars (Karen Bingham, 1 Dec 2025); no Zycus-specific lean expressed.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only - manual/paper-based sourcing, weak contract lifecycle control and contract 'leakage' (Matt Mazzotta, 9 Jul 2025; Karen, 1 Dec 2025) - with no ROI, savings or cycle-time figure quantified by the buyer.
